meta.title: Historical Events
meta.subtitle: Turning points across world history — searchable.

glossary.heading: Periods — how the historical eras are bounded

terms.Ancient (to 500 CE): From the earliest records through the fall of the western Roman empire.
terms.Medieval (500–1500): The middle ages across Europe, the Islamic world, and Asia.
terms.Early modern (1500–1800): The age of exploration, reformation, and revolutions.
terms.20th century onward: World wars, decolonisation, and the digital age.

meta.footer: A placeholder topic seeded with sample events. Dates are scholarly estimates where exact dates are unknown.

entries.name: Founding of Rome
entries.desc: Traditional date for the founding of the city of Rome, later the centre of the Roman Republic and Empire.

entries.name: Battle of Marathon
entries.desc: Athenian-led victory over the first Persian invasion of Greece, a defining moment for the Greek city-states.

entries.name: Unification of China under Qin
entries.desc: Qin Shi Huang united the warring states into the first centralised Chinese empire.

entries.name: Fall of the Western Roman Empire
entries.desc: Deposition of Romulus Augustulus, conventionally marking the end of the western Roman Empire.

entries.name: The Hijra
entries.desc: Muhammad's migration from Mecca to Medina, the starting point of the Islamic calendar.

entries.name: Norman Conquest of England
entries.desc: William of Normandy's victory at Hastings reshaped English language, law, and aristocracy.

entries.name: Fall of Constantinople
entries.desc: Ottoman capture of the Byzantine capital, often taken to mark the end of the Middle Ages.

entries.name: Columbus Reaches the Americas
entries.desc: First of Columbus's voyages, opening sustained European contact with the Americas.

entries.name: Arrival of the First Fleet
entries.desc: British ships established the first European colony in Australia at Sydney Cove.

entries.name: French Revolution Begins
entries.desc: Storming of the Bastille opened a decade of upheaval that toppled the French monarchy.

entries.name: Meiji Restoration
entries.desc: Restoration of imperial rule in Japan and the start of rapid modernisation.

entries.name: Berlin Conference
entries.desc: European powers formalised the partition and colonisation of Africa.

entries.name: Partition of India
entries.desc: Independence and division of British India into India and Pakistan.

entries.name: Apollo 11 Moon Landing
entries.desc: First crewed landing on the Moon, by the United States' Apollo programme.

entries.name: Siege of Van
entries.desc: Defense of Van (1915), the Armenian population's self-defense against Ottoman forces during the Armenian genocide.

entries.name: Battle of Fahl
entries.desc: Major battle in the Muslim conquest of Byzantine Syria, fought by Rashidun Caliphate forces near Pella (Fahl).

entries.name: Polish–Muscovite War
entries.desc: The Polish–Muscovite War (1605–18), a conflict between the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth and the Tsardom of Russia.

entries.name: Treaty of Hünkâr İskelesi
entries.desc: Treaty between the Russian Empire and the Ottoman Empire establishing a mutual defence alliance.

entries.name: Battle of Madagascar
entries.desc: Allied campaign to capture the Vichy French-controlled island of Madagascar during World War II.

entries.name: Battle of Guandu
entries.desc: Cao Cao's decisive victory over Yuan Shao's larger forces, a turning point of the late Eastern Han dynasty.

entries.name: Battle of Puebla
entries.desc: Mexican victory over French forces at Puebla during the second French intervention in Mexico.

entries.name: Battle of Auray
entries.desc: Decisive confrontation of the Breton War of Succession, part of the Hundred Years' War.

entries.name: Pig War
entries.desc: Largely bloodless 1859 border confrontation between the United States and the British Colony of Vancouver Island.

entries.name: Siege of Vicksburg
entries.desc: Final major military action of the Vicksburg campaign, ending in a decisive Union victory in the American Civil War.

entries.name: Battle of Los Angeles
entries.desc: A rumored 1942 Japanese air attack on Los Angeles triggered an anti-aircraft artillery barrage over the city.

entries.name: Battle of Rivoli
entries.desc: Napoleon's decisive defeat of Austrian forces near Rivoli, the climax of the Italian campaign of 1796–97.

entries.name: King William's War
entries.desc: North American theater of the Nine Years' War, fought between New France and New England and their Native allies.

entries.name: Battle of Smolensk
entries.desc: Battle of Smolensk (1812), fought during Napoleon's invasion of Russia.

entries.name: Battle of Vaslui
entries.desc: Stephen III of Moldavia's victory over Ottoman forces near Vaslui in 1475.

entries.name: Battle of Maipú
entries.desc: Decisive Patriot victory over Spanish royalists near Santiago, completing Chile's independence.

entries.name: Battle of Yamen
entries.desc: Naval battle marking the last stand of the Song dynasty against the invading Yuan dynasty forces.

entries.name: Battle of Neerwinden
entries.desc: Battle of Neerwinden (1793), a French defeat to Austrian forces during the War of the First Coalition.

entries.name: Siege of Bursa
entries.desc: Long Ottoman siege that ended in the capture of Bursa (Prusa) in 1326, an early Ottoman conquest.

entries.name: Battle of Fulford
entries.desc: Harald Hardrada and Tostig Godwinson's defeat of the Northern Earls near York, days before Stamford Bridge.

entries.name: Battle of Worcester
entries.desc: Oliver Cromwell's Parliamentarian army defeated Charles II's Royalist forces at Worcester, ending the Wars of the Three Kingdoms.

entries.name: Union of Krewo
entries.desc: Grand Duke Jogaila of Lithuania pledged to marry Queen Jadwiga of Poland, laying the groundwork for a Polish-Lithuanian union.

entries.name: Battle of Alcácer Quibir
entries.desc: Portuguese forces under King Sebastian were destroyed by a Moroccan army near Ksar-el-Kebir, ending Portugal's independent monarchy.

entries.name: Battle of Rorke's Drift
entries.desc: A small British garrison defended a mission station against a much larger Zulu force during the Anglo-Zulu War.

entries.name: Battle of Mons Graupius
entries.desc: Roman forces under Agricola defeated a Caledonian army in what is now Scotland, per Tacitus's account.

entries.name: Battle of Ad Decimum
entries.desc: Byzantine general Belisarius defeated the Vandal army near Carthage, opening the reconquest of North Africa.

entries.name: Great Molasses Flood
entries.desc: A ruptured storage tank released a wave of molasses through Boston's North End, killing 21 people.

entries.name: Battle of Princeton
entries.desc: George Washington's forces defeated a British rearguard at Princeton, New Jersey, boosting American morale after Trenton.

entries.name: Battle of the Camel
entries.desc: Caliph Ali defeated a rebel coalition led by Aisha, Talha, and Zubayr outside Basra, in the first civil war of Islam.

entries.name: Vandalic War
entries.desc: Byzantine forces under Justinian I reconquered North Africa from the Vandal Kingdom.

entries.name: Battle of Omdurman
entries.desc: Anglo-Egyptian forces under Kitchener crushed the Mahdist army near Omdurman, Sudan, cementing British control of the region.

entries.name: Battle of Mons Lactarius
entries.desc: Byzantine forces defeated the Ostrogoths near Mount Vesuvius, ending organized Gothic resistance in Italy.

entries.name: Battle of Annual
entries.desc: Riffian Berber forces inflicted a catastrophic defeat on the Spanish Army in northeastern Morocco, triggering political crisis in Spain.

entries.name: Smolensk War
entries.desc: The Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth fought Muscovy over control of Smolensk.

entries.name: Battle of Formigny
entries.desc: A decisive French victory over England near Formigny hastened the end of the Hundred Years' War in Normandy.

entries.name: Battle of Wake Island
entries.desc: Japanese forces captured the American-held Pacific atoll of Wake Island after a fierce two-week defense.

entries.name: Battles of Kawanakajima
entries.desc: Takeda Shingen and Uesugi Kenshin fought a series of inconclusive battles for control of the Kawanakajima plain in Sengoku-era Japan.

entries.name: Battle of Boyacá
entries.desc: The Battle of Boyacá (1819), also known as the Battle of Boyacá Bridge was a decisive victory by a combined army of Venezuelan and New Granadan troops along with a British Legion led by General Simon Bolivar over the III Division of the Spanish Expeditionary Army of Costa Firme commanded by Spanish Colonel José Barreiro.

entries.name: First Anglo-Dutch War
entries.desc: The First Anglo-Dutch War, or First Dutch War, was a naval conflict between the Commonwealth of England and the Dutch Republic, largely caused by disputes over trade.

entries.name: Second Ottoman–Venetian War
entries.desc: The Second Ottoman–Venetian War was fought from 1499 to 1503 between the Ottoman Empire and the Republic of Venice for control of contested lands in the Aegean, Ionian, and Adriatic seas.

entries.name: First Ottoman–Venetian War
entries.desc: The First Ottoman–Venetian War was fought between the Republic of Venice with its allies and the Ottoman Empire from 1463 to 1479, resulting in the loss of several Venetian holdings in Albania and Greece.

entries.name: Battle of Edessa
entries.desc: The Battle of Edessa took place between the armies of the Roman Empire under Valerian and the Sasanian Empire under Shapur I in 260, resulting in the capture of a Roman emperor for the first time.

entries.name: War of the Oranges
entries.desc: The War of the Oranges was a brief 1801 conflict in which Spanish forces, instigated and supported by France, invaded Portugal, resulting in the Treaty of Badajoz.

entries.name: Webster–Ashburton Treaty
entries.desc: The Webster–Ashburton Treaty resolved several border issues between the United States and the British North American colonies, signed in Washington, DC in 1842.

entries.name: Musket Wars
entries.desc: The Musket Wars were a series of as many as 3,000 battles and raids fought throughout New Zealand among Māori between 1806 and 1845, after Māori first obtained muskets.

entries.name: Battle of Didgori
entries.desc: The Battle of Didgori was fought between the armies of the Kingdom of Georgia and the Seljuk Empire near Tbilisi in 1121, resulting in a devastating defeat for the Seljuk-led coalition.

entries.name: Battle of Toba-Fushimi
entries.desc: The Battle of Toba–Fushimi occurred between pro-Imperial and Tokugawa shogunate forces during the Boshin War in Japan, beginning on 27 January 1868 near Kyoto.

entries.name: Battle of Orsha
entries.desc: The Battle of Orsha was fought on 8 September 1514 between the allied forces of the Grand Duchy of Lithuania and the Crown of the Kingdom of Poland and the army of the Grand Duchy of Moscow.

entries.name: Third Anglo-Burmese War
entries.desc: The Third Anglo-Burmese War was the final of three 19th-century wars between the Burmese and the British, ending in the loss of Burmese sovereignty under the Konbaung dynasty.

entries.name: Continuation War
entries.desc: The Continuation War, also known as the Second Soviet–Finnish War, was fought by Finland and Nazi Germany against the Soviet Union from 1941 to 1944, ending with the Moscow Armistice.

entries.name: Sixth Russo-Turkish War
entries.desc: The Russo-Turkish War of 1768–1774 was a major armed conflict that saw Russian armies victorious against the Ottoman Empire, bringing Crimea into the Russian sphere of influence.

entries.name: Seventh Russo-Turkish War
entries.desc: The Russo-Turkish War of 1787–1792 involved an unsuccessful attempt by the Ottoman Empire to regain lands lost to Russia in the previous Russo-Turkish War, fought alongside several concurrent European wars.

entries.name: Battle of Roncevaux Pass
entries.desc: The Battle of Roncevaux Pass in 778 saw a large force of Basques ambush part of Charlemagne's army in the Pyrenees, on the present border between France and Spain.

entries.name: Battle of Loos
entries.desc: The biggest British attack of 1915, fought on the Western Front in France and marking the war's first British use of poison gas.

entries.name: Hundred Years' War
entries.desc: A prolonged conflict between the kingdoms of England and France over rival claims to the French throne, fought intermittently from 1337 to 1453.

entries.name: Spanish–American War
entries.desc: A brief 1898 war between Spain and the United States, sparked by the sinking of the USS Maine, that ended Spanish colonial rule in Cuba, Puerto Rico, Guam, and the Philippines.

entries.name: Third Battle of Panipat
entries.desc: The Maratha Empire suffered a devastating defeat to the invading Durrani Empire near Panipat in 1761, reshaping the balance of power in northern India.

entries.name: Third Anglo-Afghan War
entries.desc: A short 1919 war in which Afghanistan's new emir declared jihad against British India, ultimately securing full Afghan independence in foreign affairs.

entries.name: Battle of Chrysopolis
entries.desc: Constantine I's decisive victory over co-emperor Licinius near Chrysopolis in 324, leaving Constantine sole ruler of the Roman Empire.

entries.name: Battle of Callinicum
entries.desc: A hard-fought 531 clash in which Belisarius's Byzantine army was checked by a Sasanian cavalry force under Azarethes.

entries.name: First Vienna Award
entries.desc: A 1938 arbitration following the Munich Agreement that forced Czechoslovakia to cede southern Slovakia and Carpathian Ruthenia to Hungary.

entries.name: Battle of Kolín
entries.desc: Austrian forces under Count von Daun defeated Frederick the Great's Prussian army near Kolín in 1757, the first major battle Frederick lost.

entries.name: Wars of the Roses
entries.desc: A series of dynastic civil wars fought between the houses of Lancaster and York for control of the English throne from 1455 to 1487.

entries.name: Battle of Neretva
entries.desc: A major 1943 Axis offensive against Yugoslav Partisan forces along the Neretva river, one of the largest confrontations of the Yugoslav theatre of World War II.

entries.name: Battle of the Ebro
entries.desc: The longest and largest battle of the Spanish Civil War, fought along the Ebro River in Catalonia between July and November 1938.

entries.name: Battle of Akroinon
entries.desc: Byzantine forces halted a major Umayyad invasion of Anatolia near Akroinon in 740, checking Arab expansion into Byzantine territory.

entries.name: Indian Mutiny of 1857
entries.desc: A widespread uprising of sepoys and civilians against East India Company rule, beginning at Meerut in 1857 and reshaping British governance of India.

entries.name: Battle of Flodden
entries.desc: An English army defeated an invading Scottish force under James IV near Flodden in 1513, part of the wider War of the League of Cambrai.

entries.name: Battle of Verdun
entries.desc: The longest battle of the First World War, fought from February to December 1916 as German forces attacked French defences north of Verdun.

entries.name: Battle of Kursk
entries.desc: The largest tank battle in history, fought in the summer of 1943 as Germany's last major offensive on the Eastern Front ended in Soviet victory.

entries.name: La Noche Triste
entries.desc: Hernán Cortés and his Spanish and native allied forces were driven out of the Aztec capital Tenochtitlan in a bloody 1520 retreat.

entries.name: Battle of the Somme
entries.desc: One of the largest and bloodiest battles of the First World War, fought by British and French forces against Germany along the Somme in 1916.

entries.name: Battle of Chickamauga
entries.desc: Confederate victory over Union forces in northwest Georgia, the most significant Union defeat in the war's Western Theater.

entries.name: Battle of Inab
entries.desc: Zengid forces under Nur al-Din Zengi destroyed the army of Prince Raymond of Antioch during the Second Crusade, killing Raymond.

entries.name: Battle of Ourique
entries.desc: Portuguese forces under Afonso Henriques defeated an Almoravid army, a battle later mythologized as founding the Kingdom of Portugal.

entries.name: Indonesian National Revolution
entries.desc: Armed and diplomatic struggle between the Republic of Indonesia and the Dutch Empire following Indonesia's 1945 declaration of independence.

entries.name: Battle of Fei River
entries.desc: Heavily outnumbered Eastern Jin forces decisively defeated the Former Qin dynasty on the Fei River, ending Fu Jian's bid to unify China.

entries.name: Treaty of Brétigny
entries.desc: Treaty between Edward III of England and John II of France that ended the first phase of the Hundred Years' War.

entries.name: Battle of Tewkesbury
entries.desc: Decisive Yorkist victory over Lancastrian forces in the Wars of the Roses, killing the Lancastrian heir Edward of Westminster.

entries.name: Battle of Evesham
entries.desc: Future King Edward I defeated and killed rebel baron Simon de Montfort, ending the Second Barons' War.

entries.name: Battle of Austerlitz
entries.desc: Napoleon's decisive victory over the combined Russian and Austrian armies, among the most significant engagements of the Napoleonic Wars.

entries.name: Battle of Lyndanisse
entries.desc: Danish forces defeated Estonian tribes during the Northern Crusades, allowing King Valdemar II to claim northern Estonia.

entries.name: Battle of Aljubarrota
entries.desc: Portuguese forces under John I decisively defeated Castile, securing Portuguese independence and John's claim to the throne.

entries.name: Third Anglo-Dutch War
entries.desc: Naval conflict between the Dutch Republic and an England allied with France, part of the wider Franco-Dutch War.

entries.name: Treaty of Verdun
entries.desc: Division of the Carolingian Empire among the three surviving sons of Louis the Pious, ending the Carolingian civil war.

entries.name: Ridda Wars
entries.desc: Campaigns launched by Caliph Abu Bakr against rebellious Arabian tribes following the death of Muhammad, consolidating the Rashidun Caliphate.

entries.name: 1920 Haiyuan earthquake
entries.desc: A catastrophic magnitude earthquake struck Haiyuan County in northwest China, one of the deadliest in history.

entries.name: 1908 Messina earthquake
entries.desc: A devastating earthquake and tsunami struck the Strait of Messina in southern Italy, killing around 120,000 people.

entries.name: 365 Crete earthquake
entries.desc: A massive undersea earthquake near Crete caused widespread destruction and tsunamis across the eastern Mediterranean.

entries.name: Winter War
entries.desc: The Soviet Union invaded Finland, which despite being outmatched inflicted heavy losses before the Moscow Peace Treaty ended the war.

entries.name: Warsaw Convention
entries.desc: International convention regulating airline liability for international carriage of passengers, baggage, and cargo.

entries.name: Polish–Russian War of 1792
entries.desc: War between the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth and the Russian Empire under Catherine the Great, allied with the Targowica Confederation.

entries.name: Battle of Muret
entries.desc: Simon de Montfort's small force of French crusaders routed a much larger army of Aragonese and Occitan lords, the decisive battle of the Albigensian Crusade.

entries.name: Italian War of 1494–1495
entries.desc: Charles VIII of France invaded Italy to press his claim to Naples, opening decades of Italian Wars among France, Spain, and the Holy Roman Empire.

entries.name: Battle of Appomattox Court House
entries.desc: Confederate general Robert E. Lee surrendered the Army of Northern Virginia to Union forces, effectively ending the American Civil War.

entries.name: 1893 Franco-Siamese crisis
entries.desc: A French gunboat standoff and ultimatum forced Siam to cede territory east of the Mekong, cementing French colonial control of Indochina.

entries.name: Battle of Talikota
entries.desc: An alliance of Deccan sultanates crushed the Vijayanagara Empire, triggering the eventual collapse of the last great Hindu empire of southern India.

entries.name: First Anglo-Maratha War
entries.desc: The British East India Company's first war with the Maratha Empire ended inconclusively after years of fighting across western India.

entries.name: Battle of Köse Dağ
entries.desc: A Mongol army decisively defeated the Seljuk Sultanate of Rum in eastern Anatolia, reducing the sultanate to Mongol vassalage.

entries.name: Battle of Bailén
entries.desc: Spanish forces forced the surrender of a French army corps, the first open-field defeat of a Napoleonic army and a spark for anti-French resistance.

entries.name: Battle of Strasbourg
entries.desc: Roman forces under Julian defeated the Alamanni confederation near modern Strasbourg, securing the Rhine frontier.

entries.name: Battle of Jerusalem
entries.desc: British Empire forces captured Jerusalem from the Ottoman Empire during World War I's Sinai and Palestine campaign.

entries.name: War of the Sixth Coalition
entries.desc: A coalition of European powers defeated Napoleonic France and forced Napoleon's abdication and exile to Elba.

entries.name: Reconquest of Constantinople
entries.desc: Nicaean forces recaptured Constantinople from the Latin Empire, restoring the Byzantine Empire under the Palaiologos dynasty.

entries.name: Battle of Velbazhd
entries.desc: Serbian forces defeated the Bulgarian Empire near Velbazhd, shifting the balance of power in the Balkans toward Serbia.

entries.name: Kashmir conflict
entries.desc: A territorial dispute between India and Pakistan over the former princely state of Jammu and Kashmir, ongoing since the 1947 partition of India.

entries.name: Battle of the Tenaru
entries.desc: Allied ground forces repelled a Japanese assault on Guadalcanal, the first major Japanese land offensive of the Pacific campaign.

entries.name: Battle of Mons
entries.desc: The British Expeditionary Force's first major engagement of World War I against the advancing German army in Belgium.

entries.name: Torstenson War
entries.desc: Sweden defeated Denmark–Norway in a conflict tied to the wider Thirty Years' War, boosting Swedish power in the Baltic.

entries.name: Russo-Persian War of 1826–28
entries.desc: Russia's final war with Qajar Iran ended in Persian territorial losses in the South Caucasus under the Treaty of Turkmenchay.

entries.name: Battle of Abritus
entries.desc: A federation of Gothic tribes defeated the Roman army and killed Emperor Decius, the first Roman emperor to die in battle against a foreign enemy.

entries.name: Battle of Cajamarca
entries.desc: Francisco Pizarro's small Spanish force ambushed and captured the Inca ruler Atahualpa, opening the Spanish conquest of the Inca Empire.

entries.name: Battle of Chacabuco
entries.desc: The Army of the Andes under José de San Martín defeated Spanish royalist forces near Santiago, a decisive early victory in the Chilean War of Independence.

entries.name: Battle of the Wilderness
entries.desc: Opening battle of Ulysses S. Grant's 1864 Overland Campaign against Robert E. Lee, fought in dense Virginia woodland during the American Civil War.

entries.name: Battle of Adrianople
entries.desc: Gothic rebels under Fritigern annihilated a Roman army and killed Emperor Valens near Adrianople, a catastrophic defeat for the Eastern Roman Empire.

entries.name: Battle of Barnet
entries.desc: Decisive Yorkist victory over Lancastrian forces in the Wars of the Roses that helped secure the English throne for Edward IV.

entries.name: War of the League of Cognac
entries.desc: Habsburg forces under Charles V fought an Italian alliance including France, the Papacy, and Venice for control of Italy from 1526 to 1530.

entries.name: Battle of Castle Itter
entries.desc: American, German, and French prisoners jointly defended an Austrian castle against the Waffen-SS in one of the final actions of World War II in Europe.

entries.name: Battle of Maldon
entries.desc: Viking raiders defeated an Anglo-Saxon force led by Earl Byrhtnoth near Maldon in Essex, later commemorated in an Old English poem.

entries.name: Treaty on the Creation of the Union of Soviet Socialist Republics
entries.desc: Declaration and treaty formally establishing the Union of Soviet Socialist Republics, centralising several Soviet republics under a Moscow-based federal government.

entries.name: Franco-Turkish War
entries.desc: Also known as the Cilicia Campaign, a conflict between France and Turkish nationalist forces from 1918 to 1921 in the aftermath of World War I.

entries.name: Battle of Gumbinnen
entries.desc: A hasty German offensive on the Eastern Front was repelled by the Russian Army near Gumbinnen in East Prussia, early in World War I.

entries.name: Umayyad conquest of Hispania
entries.desc: Umayyad forces conquered the Visigothic Kingdom on the Iberian Peninsula, ending Christian rule across most of Iberia and establishing al-Andalus.

entries.name: Anglo-Afghan Treaty of 1919
entries.desc: Also known as the Treaty of Rawalpindi, this agreement ended the Third Anglo-Afghan War and secured Afghan control over its own foreign affairs.

entries.name: Battle of Klokotnitsa
entries.desc: Bulgarian forces under Ivan Asen II crushed the Empire of Thessalonica near Klokotnitsa, restoring Bulgaria as the dominant power in southeastern Europe.

entries.name: Battle of Teruel
entries.desc: One of the bloodiest engagements of the Spanish Civil War, fought in brutal winter conditions as the city changed hands before falling to Nationalist forces.

entries.name: Battle of the Ice
entries.desc: Alexander Nevsky's Novgorodian forces defeated the Livonian Order on the frozen Lake Peipus, halting German and Danish crusading expansion into Rus' lands.

entries.name: Battle of Belleau Wood
entries.desc: American Marine and Army units, alongside French and British forces, halted the German spring offensive near the Marne River in World War I.

entries.name: Battle of Cape Esperance
entries.desc: A U.S. Navy cruiser force ambushed and defeated a Japanese squadron off Guadalcanal in the Solomon Islands, part of the wider naval campaign there.

entries.name: Battle of Alarcos
entries.desc: Almohad forces under Abu Yusuf Ya'qub al-Mansur crushed the Castilian army of Alfonso VIII, forcing a Christian retreat to Toledo.

entries.name: Battle of Stallupönen
entries.desc: The opening battle of World War I's Eastern Front, in which German forces under Hermann von François defeated a larger but disorganized Russian force.

entries.name: Polish–Lithuanian War
entries.desc: An undeclared conflict between newly independent Lithuania and Poland fought mainly around Vilnius and Suwałki in the aftermath of World War I.

entries.name: Battle of Harran
entries.desc: A major Seljuk Turk victory over the Crusader states of Antioch and Edessa near the Mesopotamian city of Harran in 1104.

entries.name: Ecuadorian–Peruvian War
entries.desc: A 1941 border war between Ecuador and Peru, the first of three 20th-century conflicts between the two nations.

entries.name: Portuguese Restoration War
entries.desc: Portugal's uprising against Habsburg Spain beginning in 1640, ending the Iberian Union and restoring Portuguese independence by 1668.

entries.name: Battle of Bapheus
entries.desc: A decisive victory for Osman I's Ottoman forces over the Byzantines in Bithynia, marking the rise of the Ottoman state.

entries.name: Battle of Leipzig
entries.desc: The largest battle of the Napoleonic Wars, in which allied Austrian, Prussian, Russian and Swedish forces decisively defeated Napoleon at Leipzig.

entries.name: Potsdam Conference
entries.desc: A meeting of Allied leaders Truman, Churchill (later Attlee), and Stalin at Potsdam to plan the postwar order after Germany's defeat.

entries.name: Battle of Moscow
entries.desc: A Soviet defensive campaign that halted the German advance on Moscow during the winter of 1941–42.

entries.name: Battle of France
entries.desc: Germany's rapid invasion and defeat of France and the Low Countries in 1940, forcing the Allied evacuation at Dunkirk.

entries.name: Great Northern War
entries.desc: A coalition led by Russia broke Swedish dominance in Northern, Central and Eastern Europe between 1700 and 1721.

entries.name: Revolt of the Comuneros
entries.desc: A Castilian uprising against the rule of Charles I, at its peak controlling the cities of Valladolid, Tordesillas, and Toledo.

entries.name: War of the Pacific
entries.desc: Chile defeated a Bolivian-Peruvian alliance over Atacama Desert resources, gaining territory from both nations.

entries.name: Operation Michael
entries.desc: The opening German offensive of the 1918 spring campaign, launched from the Hindenburg Line to break the Allied lines.

entries.name: Anti-Comintern Pact
entries.desc: An anti-communist alliance between Nazi Germany and Imperial Japan, signed in Berlin and later joined by Italy and other states.

entries.name: Nine Years' War
entries.desc: A European great-power war from 1688 to 1697 pitting France against the Grand Alliance.

entries.name: Battle of Worringen
entries.desc: The decisive battle of the War of the Limburg Succession, fought near Cologne between the Archbishop of Cologne and his rivals.

entries.name: Battle of Poltava
entries.desc: Tsar Peter I's decisive defeat of Charles XII's Swedish army, ending Sweden's status as a European great power.

entries.name: Treaty of friendship and alliance between the Government of Mongolia and Tibet
entries.desc: A 1913 agreement signed at Urga between the governments of Mongolia and Tibet.

entries.name: Battle of Raseiniai
entries.desc: A major tank battle in Lithuania during the opening days of Operation Barbarossa, between German and Soviet armored forces.

entries.name: Locarno Treaties
entries.desc: A set of post-WWI agreements among Germany, France, Britain, Belgium, Italy, Poland and Czechoslovakia guaranteeing western European borders.

entries.name: Tripartite Pact
entries.desc: A defensive military alliance between Germany, Italy, and Japan signed in Berlin, later joined by several other Axis states.

entries.name: Battle of Crete
entries.desc: German airborne invasion of Crete in May 1941, the first major airborne invasion in military history.

entries.name: Second Battle of El Alamein
entries.desc: Allied victory in Egypt that halted the Axis advance in North Africa and marked a turning point in the desert campaign.

entries.name: Austro-Prussian War
entries.desc: Short war in which Prussia defeated Austria, ending Austrian dominance in Germany and paving the way for Prussian-led unification.

entries.name: Battle of Tannenberg
entries.desc: Decisive German victory over the Russian army in East Prussia during the opening weeks of World War I.

entries.name: Battle of Angaur
entries.desc: U.S. Army assault on the Japanese-held island of Angaur in Palau during the Pacific War.

entries.name: Battle of Spotsylvania Court House
entries.desc: Bloody two-week clash between Grant's and Lee's armies in Virginia during the American Civil War's Overland Campaign.

entries.name: Hispano-Moroccan War
entries.desc: Spanish military campaign against Morocco fought around Ceuta, resulting in expanded Spanish territory in North Africa.

entries.name: Battle of Berlin
entries.desc: Final major Soviet offensive of World War II in Europe, culminating in the fall of the German capital and Hitler's suicide.

entries.name: Munich agreement
entries.desc: Agreement by Britain, France, Germany, and Italy permitting Nazi annexation of the Sudetenland, widely seen as a failed act of appeasement.

entries.name: Battle of Jena–Auerstedt
entries.desc: Napoleon's twin victories over Prussia that shattered the Prussian army and led to French occupation of Prussia.

entries.name: New Zealand Wars
entries.desc: Series of conflicts between the British Crown and Māori iwi over land and sovereignty in colonial New Zealand.

entries.name: Battle of Blenheim
entries.desc: Decisive Allied victory over French and Bavarian forces during the War of the Spanish Succession, fought in Bavaria.

entries.name: French and Indian War
entries.desc: North American theater of the Seven Years' War, fought between Britain and France, with Native American allies, for colonial control.

entries.name: Lapland War
entries.desc: Conflict in which Finland fought to expel German troops from Finnish Lapland after Finland's armistice with the Soviet Union.

entries.name: Battle of Königgrätz
entries.desc: Decisive Prussian victory over Austria in Bohemia that effectively ended the Austro-Prussian War.

entries.name: Second Schleswig War
entries.desc: War in which Prussia and Austria defeated Denmark, securing the duchies of Schleswig and Holstein for the German states.

entries.name: Battle of Seven Pines
entries.desc: Inconclusive but bloody Civil War battle near Richmond, Virginia, notable for wounding Confederate general Joseph Johnston.

entries.name: Siege of Budapest
entries.desc: Prolonged Soviet siege that captured the Hungarian capital from German and Hungarian forces in the closing months of World War II.

entries.name: Second Battle of Kharkov
entries.desc: German victory that encircled and destroyed several Soviet armies attempting to retake Kharkiv in eastern Ukraine.

entries.name: Battle of Somosierra
entries.desc: Napoleon's forces broke through a Spanish mountain pass defense on the road to Madrid during the Peninsular War.

entries.name: Battle of Narva
entries.desc: Swedish victory over a much larger Russian army at the outset of the Great Northern War in Estonia.

entries.name: Treaty of Rapallo
entries.desc: Agreement between Weimar Germany and Soviet Russia restoring diplomatic relations and renouncing wartime claims.

entries.name: Battle of Sedan
entries.desc: Decisive Prussian victory that captured Napoleon III and effectively ended the Franco-Prussian War.

entries.name: Jay's Treaty
entries.desc: Agreement easing lingering tensions between the United States and Great Britain after the Revolutionary War.

entries.name: War of the Bavarian Succession
entries.desc: Largely bloodless conflict between Austria and Prussia over the succession to the Bavarian throne.

entries.name: Third Battle of Kharkov
entries.desc: German counteroffensive that recaptured the Ukrainian city of Kharkov after the Soviet victory at Stalingrad.

entries.name: Northern Seven Years' War
entries.desc: Baltic conflict between Denmark-Norway and Sweden, ended by the Treaty of Stettin.

entries.name: Battle of Ulm
entries.desc: Napoleon's maneuvers forced the surrender of an Austrian army without a major battle, opening the road to Vienna.

entries.name: Battle of Legnica
entries.desc: Mongol forces defeated a coalition of Polish and allied knights during the Mongol invasion of Europe.

entries.name: Russo-Japanese War
entries.desc: Conflict between the Russian and Japanese empires over Manchuria and Korea, won decisively by Japan.

entries.name: Treaty of Meerssen
entries.desc: Partition of the Carolingian Middle Kingdom between the East and West Frankish rulers.

entries.name: Demyansk Pocket
entries.desc: German forces encircled by the Red Army near Demyansk were resupplied by air for months during WWII.

entries.name: Battle of Ramillies
entries.desc: Marlborough's victory over France secured much of the Spanish Netherlands during the War of the Spanish Succession.

entries.name: First Battle of El Alamein
entries.desc: Allied forces halted the Axis advance into Egypt during the North African campaign of WWII.

entries.name: Battle of Kiev
entries.desc: German encirclement of Soviet forces around Kyiv produced one of the largest encirclements of WWII.

entries.name: Battle of Hürtgen Forest
entries.desc: Prolonged and costly Allied campaign in a forested region of Germany near the Belgian border during WWII.

entries.name: Treaty of Rastatt
entries.desc: Peace agreement between France and Austria that concluded the War of the Spanish Succession.

entries.name: Potsdam Declaration
entries.desc: Allied ultimatum issued from Potsdam demanding Japan's unconditional surrender to end WWII.

entries.name: Ottoman–Safavid War
entries.desc: Conflict between the Ottoman and Safavid empires over control of Iraq and the Caucasus.
